seoTitle: How to Create a Survey in Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to build a survey in Typeform from scratch, add questions, apply logic, and analyze results to improve response rates.
intro: Whether you're gathering customer feedback or conducting market research, a well-built survey makes a real difference in the quality of insights you collect. This guide covers how to create a survey in Typeform, customize the design, apply logic, and analyze the responses once they come in.

steps.heading: Create a new survey
steps.body: Start a new Typeform and select a survey-friendly template or begin from a blank form to build your questionnaire.

steps.heading: Add questions and customize design
steps.body: Add the question types you need for your survey and adjust the theme and branding to match your organization's style.

steps.heading: Configure logic and settings
steps.body: Use conditional logic to skip or show questions based on prior answers, keeping the survey relevant to each respondent.

steps.heading: Publish and analyze responses
steps.body: Share the survey to start collecting responses, then use Typeform's results tools to review trends and insights.

faq.question: How can I improve survey response rates?
faq.answer: Keeping the survey focused with logic that skips irrelevant questions, along with a clean design, can help improve completion and response rates.

faq.question: Can I use Typeform for market research surveys?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform is commonly used for market research, employee feedback, and customer satisfaction surveys among other use cases.

faq.question: How do I analyze the results after collecting responses?
faq.answer: Typeform's Results section provides response summaries and individual answers you can review to identify trends.

faq.question: Is logic necessary for a basic survey?
faq.answer: No, logic is optional, but it becomes useful when you want different respondents to see different questions based on earlier answers.
